name: Grant County State Bank
description_short: Grant County State Bank is a locally-owned community bank established in 1939, operating three branches in Indiana with FDIC insurance and traditional banking services.
description_long:
Grant County State Bank was founded in 1939 and operates as a locally-owned community bank serving central Indiana. The institution maintains three physical branch locations in Swayzee, Sweetser, and Marion, emphasizing personal customer service and community presence. The bank is FDIC-insured, meaning deposits are backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing deposit protection up to applicable limits.

The bank offers a range of traditional banking products including personal checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), personal lending, business checking accounts, and trust services. Their featured CD rates include a 60-month CD at 3.50% APY and 54/48-month CDs at 3.25-3.29% APY. Customers can access accounts remotely through online banking and mobile apps available on both Android and iOS platforms, along with telephone banking options via local and toll-free numbers.

Grant County State Bank distinguishes itself through its emphasis on personalized, service-oriented banking in a community bank model. The institution explicitly markets itself as locally-owned and focused on customer care, with experienced professional staff at each branch. All three locations offer nightdrop services for after-hours deposits, and the bank provides flexible business and personal account options designed to meet individual customer needs.

As a traditional community bank, Grant County State Bank serves customers seeking local banking relationships and FDIC-insured deposit products. However, the bank's service area appears geographically limited to central Indiana, and current website information does not detail loan terms, fees, minimum balances, or comprehensive product comparisons. Customers should contact branches directly for specific pricing and product details before opening accounts. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal checking accounts with multiple options
Certificates of deposit (CDs) with terms ranging 48-60 months
Nightdrop deposit services at all locations
In-person banking at three branch locations
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full U.S. government protection
Locally-owned community bank operating since 1939 with established reputation
Three physical branch locations in Indiana with in-person customer service
Online banking and mobile app access available 24/7 for account management
Nightdrop services available at all three locations for secure after-hours deposits
Offers personal lending options in addition to deposit and checking products
Trust department services for specialized private account management
Competitive CD rates (3.50% APY on 60-month CDs)
cons: Limited geographic service area restricted to central Indiana locations
Phone service outages reported at Swayzee branch requiring workaround contact methods
Website lacks detailed fee schedules, minimum balance requirements, or loan terms
No information provided about credit cards, investment products, or wealth management
Limited branch hours (closes at 4:30 PM weekdays, 6 PM Friday) may not suit all customers
best_for: Central Indiana residents seeking local community banking with in-person relationships
Customers prioritizing FDIC deposit insurance and traditional banking products
Small business owners needing business checking and local lending relationships
Individuals building trust accounts or requiring estate planning services
diagnosis: Grant County State Bank is best for central Indiana residents seeking traditional community banking with FDIC-insured deposits and personal service relationships. The primary caveat is geographic limitation to three Indiana locations and lack of detailed product information on the website—prospective customers must contact branches directly for specific terms, fees, and lending requirements.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
